questions.question: Sound is produced by
questions.explanation: Sound is produced when objects vibrate.

questions.question: Sound requires ______ to travel.
questions.explanation: Sound needs a medium (solid, liquid, gas) to travel.

questions.question: The speed of sound is fastest in
questions.explanation: Sound travels fastest in solids like steel.

questions.question: Human ear can hear sound in the range
questions.explanation: Audible range for humans is 20 Hz to 20 kHz.

questions.question: The pitch of a sound depends on
questions.explanation: Higher frequency → higher pitch.

questions.question: The loudness of a sound depends on
questions.explanation: Loudness is related to amplitude of vibration.

questions.question: Ultrasonic waves are sound waves with frequency
questions.explanation: Ultrasonic waves have frequency above 20 kHz.

questions.question: Infrasonic waves are sound waves with frequency
questions.explanation: Infrasonic waves have frequency below 20 Hz.

questions.question: Echo is produced by
questions.explanation: Echo is the reflection of sound from a surface.

questions.question: The minimum distance required to hear an echo clearly is
questions.explanation: Distance must be at least 17 m (so total 2×17=34 m for sound to travel back) to hear echo.

questions.question: The phenomenon of increase in frequency when source approaches observer is called
questions.explanation: Doppler effect is change in frequency due to relative motion.

questions.question: Musical sound is characterized by
questions.explanation: Musical sound has regular vibrations and definite pitch.

questions.question: Noise is produced by
questions.explanation: Noise is irregular and unpleasant sound.

questions.question: Sonar uses
questions.explanation: Sonar uses ultrasonic waves to detect objects under water.

questions.question: Speed of sound in air at room temperature is approximately
questions.explanation: Speed of sound in air ≈ 340 m/s at 25°C.

questions.question: Which of the following can reflect sound?
questions.explanation: Hard surfaces like walls reflect sound waves.

questions.question: If the amplitude of vibration increases, the loudness
questions.explanation: Larger amplitude → louder sound.

questions.question: Vibrating strings produce sound due to
questions.explanation: Sound is produced by vibration of the string.

questions.question: Thunder is caused by
questions.explanation: Thunder is sound produced by sudden expansion of air due to lightning.

questions.question: Which type of waves are sound waves?
questions.explanation: Sound waves are longitudinal waves.

questions.question: Frequency of a wave is
questions.explanation: Frequency = number of vibrations per second, unit = Hz.

questions.question: Wavelength of sound is

options.C: Distance between two consecutive similar points

questions.explanation: Wavelength is the distance between two consecutive points in phase.

questions.question: Sound cannot travel through
questions.explanation: Sound cannot travel in vacuum due to absence of medium.

questions.question: Human voice produces
questions.explanation: Human voice can produce both musical sound (singing) and noise (shouting).

questions.question: A siren produces a high-pitched sound. This indicates
questions.explanation: High-pitched sound → high frequency.
